title: campanula lusitanica subsp. transtagana
intro: Campanula lusitanica subsp. transtagana, also known as Transtagana bellflower, is a subspecies of the Campanula lusitanica plant, a flowering perennial native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved wound healing. The herbal preparations of Campanula lusitanica subsp. transtagana typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or taken orally, depending on the specific application.
